content.question: Would delaying Hungary's tracking to age 16 (as Poland did) produce comparable attainment and labor-market gains, given Hungary's unconstrained free school-choice regime and Roma-minority residential concentration, which differ from Poland's context?
content.reduction_path: Conduct detailed institutional mapping of school-choice and segregation dynamics in both Poland (pre- and post-1999 reform) and Hungary, with matched demographic analysis, to identify which contextual factors drove Poland's reform success and whether they are replicable in Hungary
